summary: Ukrainian FPV drone operators are intensifying ambushes on Russian forces along the Pokrovsk axis, with tactics including coordinated strikes—such as hitting a Russian soldier behind a tree with two drones at once—and attempts to dismount troops from horses before attacking. Russian forces are adapting by deploying log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas," improving range, resistance to jamming, and polarization, which complicates Ukrainian interception. Meanwhile, the "Rubikon" Center reported 110 targets eliminated on March 18, 2026, across multiple axes, including anti-drone operations against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs. A notable incident saw a Russian infantryman charging a Ukrainian FPV drone with a bayonet and losing the duel. Separately, Iranian-backed militias used fiber-optic FPV drones to strike the US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport.
